description: Interim Measures for the Management of Generative AI Services — first binding regulation specifically targeting generative AI. 24 articles across 5 chapters, jointly issued by 7 agencies led by CAC.

chapters.title: General Provisions
chapters.description: Purpose, scope, definitions, and guiding principles including adherence to Core Socialist Values.

chapters.title: Technology Development and Governance
chapters.description: Requirements for training data, algorithmic design, and technology improvement.

chapters.title: Service Norms
chapters.description: Provider obligations for content review, user protection, complaint handling, and labeling.

chapters.title: Supervision and Legal Liability
chapters.description: Regulatory oversight, algorithm registration, security assessments, and penalties.

chapters.title: Supplementary Provisions
chapters.description: Effective date, scope clarifications, and transitional arrangements.

keyProvisions.name: Content Review & Core Socialist Values
keyProvisions.description: Providers must prevent generation of content undermining state power, national security, or promoting terrorism, discrimination, violence, or obscenity. Must adhere to Core Socialist Values.

keyProvisions.name: Training Data Requirements
keyProvisions.description: Data must come from lawful sources, respect intellectual property rights, and obtain consent for personal information. Must ensure authenticity, accuracy, objectivity, and diversity of training data.

keyProvisions.name: Content Labeling
keyProvisions.description: Synthetic images, videos, and other generated content must carry identification labels (watermarks/metadata) per Deep Synthesis Management Regulations.

keyProvisions.name: Algorithm Registration & Security Assessment
keyProvisions.description: Services with public opinion attributes or social mobilization capacity must undergo security assessments and comply with algorithm registration requirements.

keyProvisions.name: User Protection
keyProvisions.description: Providers must safeguard user input and usage records, prohibit unnecessary personal data collection, take measures to prevent excessive use by minors.

keyProvisions.name: Territorial Scope
keyProvisions.description: Applies to services generating text, images, audio, video, or other content provided to the public in mainland PRC. Article 20 addresses extraterritorial application.

penalties.summary: Penalties follow existing Chinese cybersecurity and data security laws — warnings, fines, service suspension, criminal liability for severe violations.
